steps.title: Find the local contrast
steps.content: The claim says Harper criticizes activists who champion humanitarian causes in other countries while overlooking local concerns. The best quotation must therefore contain a contrast between attention to distant suffering and neglect of suffering nearby.

steps.title: Eliminate broad appeals
steps.content: Choice A discusses divine justice against those who harm the poor and lowly, but it does not contrast foreign and local causes. Choice C blesses a native land and hopes it stands for truth and liberty, which is patriotic rather than critical of selective activism. Choice D describes making songs for the people, not activists overlooking nearby suffering.

steps.title: Match distant and nearby
steps.content: Choice B tells listeners that when they plead for exiles from far-distant shores, they should remember bloodshed around their own doors. That directly illustrates the claim because it criticizes concern for people abroad when local suffering is being ignored. The answer is B.
